title: Barts Friend Falls In Love

jokes.text: What the..? D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer encounters some unexpected situation (likely physical mishap based on Raiders theme music)
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase is perfectly character-integrated and economical, though not original by this point in the series

jokes.text: Cool-- An oversize novelty billiard ball. You shake it up and it tells the future.
jokes.setup: Milhouse describes his new acquisition from Circus of Values
jokes.punchline: The mundane description of a Magic 8-Ball as an 'oversize novelty billiard ball'
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's innocent, overly technical description is charming character comedy

jokes.text: Will I pass my English test? ''Outlook not so good.'' Wow, it does work!
jokes.setup: Milhouse tests the Magic 8-Ball's accuracy
jokes.punchline: He's impressed by its 'accuracy' when it predicts his failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic setup - Milhouse validates the 8-Ball precisely because it confirms his academic inadequacy

jokes.text: Will... I get beat up today? ''All signs point to yes.'' That ball knows everything.
jokes.setup: Following Milhouse's validation of the 8-Ball
jokes.punchline: Bart asks about getting beat up and accepts the grim prediction with admiration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Milhouse's academic failure to Bart's physical peril, both accepted matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Will Milhouse and I be friends when we're high school dropouts living off Uncle Sucker?
jokes.setup: Bart's series of increasingly pessimistic future scenarios
jokes.punchline: 'Uncle Sucker' as slang for the government/welfare system
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their bleak future, with clever slang for government dependency

jokes.text: Yeah, sure, and they told me I'd get a big parade when I got back from 'Nam. Instead, they spat on me. I can still feel it searing.
jokes.setup: Skinner responds to Samantha claiming to be a good student
jokes.punchline: His bitter Vietnam War reference and lingering trauma
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - his Vietnam trauma inappropriately surfaces in routine school interaction

jokes.text: I can still feel it searing.
jokes.setup: Skinner mentions being spat on
jokes.punchline: He's still traumatized by symbolic spit decades later
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Skinner's ongoing psychological damage from relatively minor historical slight.

jokes.text: I see you beat that bed-wetting problem. Don't worry. They'll forget... just like they forgot about me in that tiger cage for 18 agonizing months.
jokes.setup: Skinner reading Samantha's school records
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately sharing her embarrassing history then comparing it to his POW experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Skinner's professional inappropriateness escalated by trauma comparison

jokes.text: Every night I wake up screaming. Well, let's meet your classmates.
jokes.setup: Skinner describing his PTSD symptoms
jokes.punchline: Casually transitions to normal school business
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. The abrupt tonal shift from trauma to mundane school routine is expertly handled.

jokes.text: Great.Just keep packing them in, Seymour. We should discuss this later.
jokes.setup: Another new student being introduced to an already overcrowded class
jokes.punchline: Krabappel's sarcastic complaint about overcrowding
jokes.explanation: Standard teacher frustration joke, competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: Please say a big elementary school hello to Samantha Stinky. Stanky. Oh, right. How embarrassing for you. Well, good-bye.
jokes.setup: Skinner introduces the new student
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces her name as 'Stinky,' then awkwardly exits after correcting himself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Skinner's social awkwardness and quick escape after the embarrassing mistake

jokes.text: He came here because of the high crime rate and lackluster police force.
jokes.setup: Samantha explains why her family moved to Springfield
jokes.punchline: Her father's security business benefits from Springfield's dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation about Springfield's problems being good for business

jokes.text: All my friends are back in Phoenix and this town has a weird smell that you're all probably used to but I'm not. It takes about six weeks.
jokes.setup: Samantha continues her honest assessment of Springfield
jokes.punchline: Krabappel's matter-of-fact confirmation about the smell adjustment period
jokes.explanation: Excellent mundane dystopia humor - the town's smell is so normalized they have a timeline for adjustment

jokes.text: When I find out who hit me with ball I'm going to tear them a new..!
jokes.setup: Jimbo threatens revenge for getting hit
jokes.punchline: His threat is cut off mid-curse, presumably by seeing Samantha
jokes.explanation: Standard interrupted threat gag, functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Hello. I'm actor Troy McClure. You kids might remember me from such educational films as Lead Paint:: Delicious But Deadly and Here Comes The Metric System.
jokes.setup: Troy McClure introduces himself in the sex ed film
jokes.punchline: His absurd previous film titles, especially 'Lead Paint: Delicious But Deadly'
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure formula executed perfectly - the lead paint title is darkly absurd and memorable

jokes.text: Boring.
jokes.setup: Educational film about bunny puberty and wholesome dating
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt dismissal of the sanitized content
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - single word captures Bart's disdain for sanitized education. High character integration but limited originality.

jokes.text: Eww! She's faking it.
jokes.setup: Kids watching the sanitized sex ed film's honeymoon scene
jokes.punchline: Bart's inappropriately sophisticated observation about the bunny's performance
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly subversive - Bart's adult cynicism applied to children's educational material

jokes.text: Nine months later Fluffy gave birth to 14 beautiful bunnies. Eight survive.
jokes.setup: The educational film concludes with the birth announcement
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that 6 of the 14 baby bunnies died
jokes.explanation: Darkly brilliant - the educational film's sanitized presentation casually mentions infant mortality

jokes.text: Because Mr. Krabappel chased something fluffy down a rabbit hole.
jokes.setup: Student asks why she doesn't live with her husband
jokes.punchline: Her bitter metaphor referencing the bunny sex ed film
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that transforms the innocent bunny metaphor into bitter adult reality

jokes.text: Most of you will never fall in love and marry out of fear of dying alone.
jokes.setup: Student asks how they'll know when they fall in love
jokes.punchline: Krabappel's brutally cynical response about settling for fear-based relationships
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krabappel character moment - her bitter life experience projected onto innocent children

jokes.text: God, shmod-- I want my monkey man.
jokes.setup: Krabappel says creating half-man half-monkey would be 'playing God'
jokes.punchline: Ralph dismisses theological concerns for his bizarre desire
jokes.explanation: Peak Ralph Wiggum - dismissing divine authority for absurd personal desire. 'God, shmod' is perfectly crafted dismissal.

jokes.text: God, shmod-- I want my monkey man.
jokes.setup: Martin asks about creating half-man, half-monkey creatures, teacher says that's playing God
jokes.punchline: Martin dismissively rejects religious concerns for his scientific ambitions
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of Martin's character - the nerdy kid reveals mad scientist ambitions

jokes.text: My girlfriend's dancing topless at the airport bar from 4:15 to 4:20.
jokes.setup: Bart protests Otto leaving without Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Otto's very specific time window for his girlfriend's performance
jokes.explanation: Good Otto character moment - the absurdly specific scheduling of his seedy entertainment

jokes.text: Got any girl comics like Bonnie Crane, Girl Attorney Punkin and Dunkin, the Twinkle Twins or Li'l Knee Socks?
jokes.setup: Samantha asks for girl-oriented comics
jokes.punchline: The absurd titles of made-up female comic series
jokes.explanation: Decent parody of gendered marketing in children's media, though not particularly sharp

jokes.text: No, but my sister's got a wide selection of crappy comics.
jokes.setup: Samantha asks for girl comics
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive description of Lisa's presumably higher-quality reading material
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's dismissive attitude toward Lisa's intellectual interests

jokes.text: That excess blubber could fill two-fifths of the Grand Canyon. That may not sound impressive but keep in mind, it is a very big canyon.
jokes.setup: TV report on American obesity statistics
jokes.punchline: Kent's awkward attempt to make the statistic sound more impressive
jokes.explanation: Good satire of TV journalism's attempts to sensationalize statistics

jokes.text: But in real life Santa would be suffering from gall stones hypertension, impotence, and diabetes.
jokes.setup: TV segment about jolly fat men in popular culture
jokes.punchline: The medical reality of Santa's obesity
jokes.explanation: Dark humor applying medical reality to beloved fictional character

jokes.text: I wish they had never invented fried cheese.
jokes.setup: Homer watching obesity documentary
jokes.punchline: His regret is about food invention, not his own choices
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's avoidance of personal responsibility. Blaming innovation rather than behavior is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: We call it the Good Morning Burger.
jokes.setup: Description of an extremely unhealthy burger with bacon, ham, and fried egg
jokes.punchline: The ironically cheerful name for this artery-clogging breakfast item
jokes.explanation: Standard fast food parody, competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: Next, your Mickey Mantle for my picture of Homer on the couch.
jokes.setup: Bart trading baseball cards with Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Bart values a random photo of his father equally to a Mickey Mantle card
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor - Bart's complete misunderstanding of collectible value is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Well, you kind of sort of are.
jokes.setup: Samantha says she needs to go, dad thinks she's having braces examined
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's awkward acknowledgment of their oral contact
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay about kissing/braces examination. Shows Milhouse's awkward attempts at being clever about romance.

jokes.text: Well, you kind of sort of are.
jokes.setup: Samantha says her dad thinks she's having her braces examined
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's innocent but suggestive observation about their kissing
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo delivered with Milhouse's characteristic awkwardness

jokes.text: You know, like when you open an Eskimo Pie and you wait just a little for it to melt? But she doesn't melt. Oh, yes, she does.
jokes.setup: Milhouse tries to explain the appeal of romantic anticipation
jokes.punchline: His innocent food metaphor that sounds surprisingly sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Milhouse's innocent metaphor reveals unexpected romantic sophistication

jokes.text: What do you say to that? Give me the weight loss tape.
jokes.setup: Lisa offers Homer various self-improvement options including hostage negotiations
jokes.punchline: Homer chooses weight loss despite just being interested in hostage negotiation
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Homer's distractibility

jokes.text: Would he like to lose weight, stop smoking learn the state capitals, master hostage negotiations..? Hmm... hostage negotiations.
jokes.setup: List of self-improvement tape options
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal to bizarre, Marge considers the strangest option
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list from mundane to absurd. Marge's consideration of hostage negotiation is a nice character touch.

jokes.text: We're out of ''Subliminally Slim.'' Uh... send them the vocabulary builder.
jokes.setup: Homer ordered a weight loss tape
jokes.punchline: The store substitutes a vocabulary tape instead
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the vocabulary subplot - the casual substitution sets up future confusion

jokes.text: Lamentably, no. My gastronomic rapacity knows no satiety.
jokes.setup: Marge asks if the weight loss tape has reduced his appetite
jokes.punchline: Homer uses pretentious vocabulary to say he's still hungry
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the vocabulary tape setup - Homer's flowery language contrasts hilariously with his simple message

jokes.text: No one has sat next to me since I successfully lobbied to have the school day extended by 20 minutes.
jokes.setup: Bart sits next to Martin on the bus
jokes.punchline: Martin's proud achievement that made him even more unpopular
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character moment - his academic enthusiasm creating social consequences he doesn't understand

jokes.text: Forbearance is the watchword. That triumvirate of twinkies overwhelmed my resolve.
jokes.setup: Marge comments on Homer eating three desserts
jokes.punchline: Homer's pretentious explanation for his lack of self-control
jokes.explanation: Continues the vocabulary tape payoff - 'triumvirate of Twinkies' is particularly clever alliteration

jokes.text: Here in the boudoir the gourmand metamorphosizes into... the voluptuary!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be romantic with Marge
jokes.punchline: Uses pretentious vocabulary for crude romantic advance
jokes.explanation: Great extension of Homer's vocabulary obsession into romantic context. The pretentious delivery makes it more awkward.

jokes.text: Here in the boudoir the gourmand metamorphosizes into... the voluptuary!
jokes.setup: Homer continues using fancy vocabulary
jokes.punchline: His pretentious romantic overture to Marge
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the vocabulary joke with uncomfortable romantic implications

jokes.text: What in God's name are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Homer's pretentious romantic advance
jokes.punchline: Marge's bewildered response to his vocabulary overload
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response from Marge. Her confusion is the audience's confusion, making Homer's pretensions more absurd.

jokes.text: Listen, Bart, there's such a thing as spending too much time together.
jokes.setup: Bart suggests they spit on the principal's car
jokes.punchline: Martin politely rejects delinquent behavior with diplomatic excuse
jokes.explanation: Shows Martin's diplomatic way of avoiding Bart's bad influence while maintaining politeness. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Samantha and Milhouse sitting in a tree About to lose their privacy
jokes.setup: Traditional playground rhyme about romantic couples
jokes.punchline: Twisted ending threatening to expose their relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on childhood rhyme that sets up Bart's betrayal. The meter works well and the threat is clear.

jokes.text: Now there's a Machiavellian countenance. Ooh, a sextet of ale!
jokes.setup: Homer observes Bart looking devious, then sees beer
jokes.punchline: His vocabulary-enhanced observations of everyday items
jokes.explanation: 'Sextet of ale' for a six-pack is particularly amusing vocabulary enhancement

jokes.text: Let's just say I'm concerned prude with lot of time on his hands.
jokes.setup: Bart makes anonymous call to Samantha's father
jokes.punchline: His description of himself as the anonymous tipster
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor - Bart's self-aware description while maintaining his cover

jokes.text: Well, 'tis better to have loved and lost... yatta, yatta, yatta.
jokes.setup: Bart tries to console Milhouse after losing Samantha
jokes.punchline: His dismissive truncation of the famous quote
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's impatience with sentiment

jokes.text: We started out like Romeo and Juliet but it ended up in tragedy.
jokes.setup: Milhouse reflects on his relationship with Samantha
jokes.punchline: His obliviousness that Romeo and Juliet IS a tragedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic misunderstanding - Milhouse's literary ignorance creates perfect dramatic irony

jokes.text: We started out like Romeo and Juliet but it ended up in tragedy.
jokes.setup: Milhouse lamenting his lost love
jokes.punchline: Doesn't realize Romeo and Juliet IS a tragedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Milhouse unwittingly describes exactly what Romeo and Juliet is. Perfect character moment showing his romantic naivety.

jokes.text: Disingenuous mountebanks with their subliminal chicanery-- A pox on them!
jokes.setup: Homer discovers he gained weight instead of losing it
jokes.punchline: His elaborate vocabulary-enhanced curse
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff - Homer's anger expressed through unnecessarily complex vocabulary is hilarious

jokes.text: Five fingers? Ooh-- Freak show!
jokes.setup: Martin reads about humans eventually having an extra finger
jokes.punchline: Bart's reaction to having five fingers (when humans already have five)
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor - Bart misunderstands the premise but reacts predictably

jokes.text: You cry when they're out of chocolate milk. You cry when you're doing long division and you have a remainder left over.
jokes.setup: Milhouse says he doesn't want Bart to see him cry
jokes.punchline: Bart lists Milhouse's frequent crying triggers
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - the math remainder detail is particularly specific and funny

jokes.text: You cry when they're out of chocolate milk. You cry when you're doing long division and you have a remainder left over.
jokes.setup: Milhouse says he doesn't want Bart to see him cry
jokes.punchline: Bart lists increasingly trivial reasons Milhouse cries
jokes.explanation: Good escalating structure showing Milhouse's sensitivity. The math remainder detail is particularly specific and funny.

jokes.text: Milhouse is out bed and full beans.
jokes.setup: Homer observes Milhouse's energetic reaction to Bart's confession
jokes.punchline: Homer's garbled version of 'out of bed and full of beans'
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer malapropism, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Marge... where's that... metal dealie... you use to... dig... food? You mean, a spoon?
jokes.setup: Homer struggling to remember basic vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Can't remember the word 'spoon' despite his recent vocabulary obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Homer's vocabulary has completely collapsed. The contrast with his earlier pretension is beautifully crafted.

jokes.text: You mean, a spoon? Yeah, yeah, yeah!
jokes.setup: Homer struggles to remember the word for a basic utensil
jokes.punchline: His excitement when Marge provides 'spoon'
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing the vocabulary effects wearing off, though not particularly strong

jokes.text: Samantha, ne pas des boys.
jokes.setup: Nun catches Samantha with Milhouse
jokes.punchline: Her broken French-English warning about boys
jokes.explanation: Mild stereotype humor about French-Canadian nuns, competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: It's 50 rosaries a kiss.
jokes.setup: Samantha hesitates about kissing Milhouse goodbye
jokes.punchline: The specific religious punishment for romantic contact
jokes.explanation: Good religious school humor - the specific 'price' for affection

jokes.text: It's 50 rosaries a kiss. Oh, what the heck.
jokes.setup: Samantha explains the punishment for kissing
jokes.punchline: Decides it's worth the severe religious penance
jokes.explanation: Good cost-benefit analysis humor. The specific punishment makes the choice more meaningful and funny.

jokes.text: They all look alike to me. Now, let's go whip donuts at old people.
jokes.setup: Milhouse asks if he can find another girl like Samantha
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive response followed by suggestion for cruel entertainment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart ending - his emotional detachment and immediate pivot to mischief

tmdb_overview: Bart resents Milhouse's new girlfriend; Homer gets the wrong subliminal tape, which has him gaining vocabulary, not losing weight.

guest_stars.name: Maggie Roswell
guest_stars.character: Luann Van Houten / Operator / Singing Nun (voice)

insight: Sixty rapid-fire jokes sustain an 80+ score despite modest individual impact.
